evaluation.summary: The variance-to-mean ratio of primorial gaps exhibits a statistically significant power-law increase with exponent ≈2.6 for k up to 8, challenging earlier expectations of slower growth.
next_directions: Extend the analysis to larger primorial gaps (k>12) to assess scaling stability.
Compare the fitted power-law exponent with theoretical predictions from probabilistic number theory.
Investigate alternative scaling models (e.g., logarithmic, stretched exponential) using rigorous model-selection criteria.
